item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus Christ, I wasn't supposed to be getting emotional at 8 in the morning, this was supposed to be a haha 69 article about nuts and fish.

+1 and either you or I need to see a therapist after this one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article may be a shitpost but it's a good shit post nonetheless.
+1 for rainbow Pistachio trout.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly the kind of chatlogs that I like when it comes to GAW. Honestly, it's probably my favourite aspect of GAW pieces, because they sounds so REAL sometimes. All the mistimed messages, the interruptions, all of it is really natural stuff and I couldn't ask for anything more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In other entries, they're ironic and internet-savvy, but, as far as I've seen, never internally abusive.

People are different. People change. I think the ironic internet-savvy side is far, far less important than the face that these are young kids. Like, finding their purpose in life young. I wanted to write a story about kids being cruel. GAW was a good needle to thread that narrative through.

Frankly, I think it’s silly to say that the characters are too mean-spirited. Compared to who? The groups of completely different individuals of a different nation and of different backgrounds? A far less tight-knit group in which rinsing one another has more consequences than just getting rinsed back?

There is no good one. They are not good people, really. At least I don’t intend them to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: my shitpost got +100 wow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have mixed feelings about this one.

The positive: I don't think the ending is unwarranted. It may seem out of left field to some, but the point of the SCP is detailing how what appears to be harmless ribbing to some can be emotionally traumatic to others. This feels like a very authentic portrayal of that and I do have a soft spot for SCPs that appear to be about some supernatural oddity and are actually about a horror closer to home.

The negative: I think this is hugely out of character for GAW and misunderstands who they are as a group. In other entries, they're ironic and internet-savvy, but, as far as I've seen, never internally abusive. As Edapurg said, this seems too mean-spirited for them. While earlier articles on them draw from ironic lefty internet spaces and "Weird Twitter", this article has them bouncing back and forth between that and something closer to chan culture which is a whole different area of the internet. It's hard to picture who these people are that would use the Communist Manifesto as a symbol but where even the "good one" is using "gay" as a pejorative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, this reminds me a lot of various chats I've been in, with one person whose an absolute piece of shit to others but is kept around because they're friends with the moderators, so the ending's not exactly out of left-field for me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck.

edit: actually it can stay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I find hilarious is that this SCP's name is still "le epic trollface" like a month after April Fools.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In light of the comments regarding 3169’s dated and absurdist title, I’ve finally decided to change it to something far more punchy and appropriate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t really know… the chatlogs don’t quite feel like GAW to me. They’re too mean-spirited and I don’t see much beyond that; it all comes off as general assholery without the self-aware ridiculousness. Maybe it’s just that none of them are established characters (except Bones, which makes their appearance feel like a cameo), and with such a character-focussed GOI that’s s bit jarring. Nothing wrong with that of course and it all comes down to personal preference, so I guess it’s a no vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this finna nut is finna woke +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit that twist. Upvote hard man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The title alone give you a +1. The story was enjoyable too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really loved the characterization here. Excellent work.

+1

item.title: "Finna nut"
item.content:encoded:
thought it was a joke post. this feed back is nuts

hhhhhaaaaa~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would assume 3, bones probably shows the join message intentionally, he's always watching even if he's not in the channel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The chain of events here just seems really unnecessarily cruel, especially for GAW who usually seem to be a more humane GOI, no vote

item.title: *slow standing clap*
item.content:encoded:
I never expected this GoI to be anything besides memes. But then you've gone and done it.
+1 all the way, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is interesting but like… Weirdly humorous at the same time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i use discord a lot, and this was really powerful for some reason. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't really say any of the characters in this clicked with me. Unlike pat GAW characters I've read about there was nothing that really stuck with me here, and the sudden tragedy came out of left field with too little characterization to make it work. Also, as others have said, the whole "GAW stuff ends in sadness" trope has been played out a ton to the point where it can almost be expected.

Novoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you've more or less gotten it down, based on me being in servers with internet stoners/gamers/both (see, me). I think papertrail44 is like… too much? If there were one specific element of this that would sound fake-ish, it's probably them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading some of the comments makes it sound like some of these characters are previously established… I obviously don't know any of them, and I shouldn't need to in order to find a skip successful.

Bones is the only pre-established character, and even then their role was deliberately limited to understanding little beyond its role as a moderator. Apologies if that wasn't clear.

and I feel like a lot of the chat log was overly fake gamer/internet/stoner-speak.

Would you be able to point out any specific portions that you felt were too much? It would help me avoid doing something similarly over-the-top in the future.

Thanks for checking it out anyways!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

…after much deliberation.

+ Show
- Hide

I decided to downvote. I don't care for uninspiring anomalies that do very little on their own, and rely entirely on the impact from a tangential story. Even still, that tangential story isn't all that great. Yeah, the death was a bit shocking, but I guess it felt unearned. I never cared for these characters, I didn't enjoy reading their stupid argument, and I feel like a lot of the chat log was overly fake gamer/internet/stoner-speak. Reading some of the comments makes it sound like some of these characters are previously established… I obviously don't know any of them, and I shouldn't need to in order to find a skip successful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The presence of join/leave messages at all rules out Discord. My personal headcanon is that gaycopmp4 made them some sort of IRC client that's enhanced with automatic encryption and some bonus features, such as being able to read the backscroll when you enter a channel.

… of course, this would also contradict my own articles where people ask for logs of when they weren't in the channel. It could be that some people have bouncers set up for a normal IRC client, but those people wouldn't have join/leave messages.

The simplest possibility is that someone in the channel summoned bones by sending it the logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From the logs, I'm pretty sure that they are using some sort of IRC chat. I don't know if there's some way to view previous messages, but if not, how would bones know what was going on to respond like that?

1) They use Discord and that's probably not something that would feasibly be in there.

2) Discord/IRC hybrid (which is basically what's implied heavily).

3) bones somehow knows. Would make sense; it IS a planetoid that can talk through chat programs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This didn't really feel like it was treading new ground. Sort of a cross between Mr. Ominous and It's Not Always Funny and Mr. Hax, without the heart the first two had. I dunno.

I want to second this as my own reason for no-voting in what was otherwise a pretty solid skip- this story has been done, and done specifically with GAW, before. I'd say "man, how many tragically dead members of GAW are there?", but there is no canon, so that complaint isn't really valid. I like almost everything else about this, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked this all the way through, was impressed by the ending. This is a SCP and a tale in a nutshell*.

Still liking bones as a character. If my sons actually interacted with other yoots online, I'd like there to be a bones supervising. The GAW approach to anart feels very true to life, in ways both entertaining and horrifying.

*Succumbed to temptation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm. Thank you. I'll bear that in mind next time I take a shot at one of these- I did have a blast writing the logs. It's like transcribing the conversations me and my own friends have, in places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This didn't really feel like it was treading new ground. Sort of a cross between Mr. Ominous and It's Not Always Funny and Mr. Hax, without the heart the first two had. I dunno.

That said, though? You definitely have a talent for writing the banter, and there were a few bits of dialogue that I outright enjoyed. Keep at it — I look forward to any future GAW articles you might write.

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: Seconding this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you had my upvote at the first pressing of F to pay respects. +1. F to anyone who downvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. You made Gamers against Weed dangerous without forcing it or derailing their characters. Not that they need to be dangerous or antagonistic, but I like the reminder that even the best online communities can be abusive sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: O hey, I remember reviewing this. Glad it's doing well! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
~ bones has joined the channel.
bones: Knock it off, Crooke. That's an official warning.

From the logs, I'm pretty sure that they are using some sort of IRC chat. I don't know if there's some way to view previous messages, but if not, how would bones know what was going on to respond like that?

In all entirety, I don't find this too interesting, and like weryl said above, the characterization of the two feels unnatural to me. So, gonna leave it as a no-vote.

This isn't doing it for me. The anomaly itself is pretty bland on its own (there's Anomalous Items that have more razzle dazzle), so the whole article hinges on the chat log. But for me, said log boils down to a confrontation between two rather unlikable characters and ends in a clumsy attempt to milk sympathy through one's accidental death. Surely it would take far less effort for cola to put crooke on /ignore (or whatever the GAWchat equivalent is) than to literally give himself thick skin.

To your credit, it was slightly refreshing that you broke the trend of "GAW are perfect, Chaotic Good people who can do no harm", but that's not enough to sway the vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I vaguely want to dislike GAW, in a hipsterish sort of way. But these dialogue-driven skips keep being so damn enjoyable. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding this. The end kinda comes out of left field. It's not built towards particularly well. I would say it seems out-of-character, but I didn't really get a sense that cola had a strong sense of character to begin with, so it's just a thing that happens - and I'm not nearly emotionally invested enough for it to have much impact. Couple this with the final log, which kinda just…trails off pointlessly with no real reflection on the situation or behavior that pushed this character into toying with shit beyond his control, and I don't feel like there's any kind of resolution, just empty words.

I would pull back on some of the inane chatter which is consuming the bulk of the logs and try to interject some more direction or development for cola, because it's nit quite as focused as some of the other GAW interactions I've seen. It's a fine line between silly bs and doing things like revealing character and progressing story in chat logs, but I think it's tipping too far into trying to appear like a chat room discussion - so that those elements to make it a satisfying story have been sacrificed somewhat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pistachio nuts that turn into fish and lead to someone getting killed? Well, that's something I've never seen before. Have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this as a draft and you definitely ironed out the couple of wrinkles I saw there. This is a perfect addition to GAW in my opinion (it seems very in line with what they're all about, helping to build my interest in them while still standing on its own), and I'm surprised to see it's your first shot at writing about them. Well-deserved +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: god this is amazing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Digging it, and heck, very topical for our "Internet forum generation" in general.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 👉🏻👉🏻gotcha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's cool. Thanks for giving it your time regardless. I hope you'll find more interest in my future projects. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bitter addresses that here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as I loved the skip and did very much upvote it, if the pistachios turn into fish when the bag is opened, how did we get a picture of the pistachios themself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, "finna nut" as in. Fish have fins. And the nuts turn into fish with big fins. Okay then.

I can't say I was attached or even intrigued by a single character here, the death at the end seems sudden, and it just washed over me. I didn't really say this in your draft thread because outside of radically working your chatlogs, it wouldn't have gotten me at all. So, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got that "on the verge of tears" feeling that I got when I read the draft. +1

item.title: How in the...
item.content:encoded:
You created an SCP about pistachios that turn into fish… that was made by Gamers Against Weed….

That's actually entertaining to read.

How and why I will not ask. Take your goddamn +1.

item.title: *Claps*
item.content:encoded:
In-fucking-credible. Excellent work with this skip! Love it when GAW shit goes wrong or ends up escalating (which is most of the time).

+1'd so damn hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With thanks to, in no particular order:

kinchtheknifeblade Leveritas
Sir Baubius Moklin
Decibelles fieldstone and jishwa
for their help and support.

Always wanted to take a stab at a GAW skip. Hope this one sticks.

IMAGE SOURCE: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:ARS_pistachio.jpg
